summary: In this brief update post, Joel Spolsky defends his choice to use Visual Basic despite its reputation as an uncool language, arguing that productivity matters more than syntax aesthetics. He provides updates on CityDesk beta testing, explaining delays are due to bug fixing to avoid duplicate feedback. He also mentions refinements to his discussion board, including anti-spam measures for email addresses.
key_points: Visual Basic's lack of curly braces makes developers feel uncool, but productivity should outweigh peer perception
CityDesk beta releases are being deliberately delayed to fix known bugs before expanding the tester pool
Discussion board improvements include protecting user email addresses from spam harvesters
money_quotes: The worst part about coding in Visual Basic is that people think you're not cool because your code doesn't have {'s and }'s.
I can live with the shame if it means I'm more productive.
key_insight: Choosing a programming language for productivity over perceived coolness is a pragmatic trade-off worth making.
